specification.topic-statement: The model selection subsystem configures which LLM model, provider, and thinking level an agent uses, with changes taking effect on the next request.

scope.in-scope: The /model command (model name, thinking level parsing, provider inference), the /system command (system prompt override), state changes on model switch (provider invalidation, token cache reprune), and persistence of model configuration.
scope.boundary: How the selected model and thinking level are encoded into provider-specific API request bodies is covered by the provider serialization specification.
How API keys are resolved for the inferred provider is covered by the credential loading specification.

field.name: provider
field.note: inferred from the model name; one of: anthropic, openai, google

field.name: thinking level
field.note: one of: min (disabled), low, med, high; defaults to min

behavior.model-command:
The `/model` command accepts an argument of the form `` or `/`. The forward slash separates the model name from the optional thinking level.
1. **Argument parsing**: the argument is split at the first `/`. If no `/` is present, the thinking level defaults to minimum (disabled). 2. **Provider inference**: the provider is determined from the model name by recognizing provider-specific name patterns. The three recognized providers are Anthropic (Claude models), Google (Gemini models), and OpenAI (all other models). 3. **Thinking level validation**: the level string must be one of `min`, `low`, `med`, or `high`. An unrecognized string is an error. 4. **Guard**: the command cannot execute while an LLM request is in progress. If the agent is not idle, an error is returned. 5. **State update**: the agent's provider name, model name, and thinking level are updated in memory. The cached provider instance is invalidated so a fresh one is created on the next request. The token cache is reprune to account for the new model's token counting behavior. 6. **Persistence**: the new configuration is written to the agent's database record. Persistence failures are logged as warnings; memory is authoritative.
Calling `/model` without an argument is an error.
behavior.system-command:
The `/system` command sets a session-level system prompt override that supplements or replaces the system prompt sources used during LLM request assembly:
- With a text argument: the argument string is stored as the override system message. No length or content validation is applied. - Without an argument: any existing override is cleared.
The system message override is stored in the shared configuration context, not on the individual agent. It applies globally to all agents in the session.
Unlike model changes, the system message override set via `/system` is not persisted to the database. It is lost when the session ends.
behavior.provider-invalidation: When the model or provider changes, the cached provider instance (which holds the configured API endpoint, authentication, and model parameters) is destroyed. The next LLM request will instantiate a new provider instance with the updated configuration. This ensures that no stale provider configuration is used after a model switch.
